title: General Administration
summary: Governance, purpose, scope, and the rules for adopting and maintaining the NCS within an organization.
description: The administrative module explains how the National CAD Standard is structured, how to reference it in contracts and specifications, how to document organization-specific deviations, and how the consensus maintenance process works. It is the entry point every firm reads first.

title: Uniform Drawing System (UDS)
summary: The core of the standard: sheet organization, drawing set order, schedules, notations, and symbols.
description: The Uniform Drawing System defines eight integrated modules covering sheet identification, sheet organization, schedules, drafting conventions, terms and abbreviations, symbols, notations, and code conventions. This is the most-used reference on any drafting team.

title: AIA CAD Layer Guidelines
summary: The discipline-based layer naming system that keeps every layer predictable across firms and platforms.
description: The AIA CAD Layer Guidelines specify a structured layer name format — discipline designator, major group, minor group, and status field — so that layers are self-describing and consistent. This module is the backbone of cross-firm CAD collaboration.

title: Plotting Guidelines
summary: Line weights, pen tables, color-to-weight mapping, and consistent, reproducible plotted output.
description: The Plotting Guidelines establish how on-screen colors map to plotted line weights, recommended pen tables, and conventions that guarantee a drawing plotted in one office matches the appearance of the same drawing plotted in another.

title: BIM Implementation
summary: How NCS conventions map onto model-based delivery, object naming, and BIM execution planning.
description: New in Version 7, the BIM Implementation module bridges traditional 2D CAD conventions and model-based workflows. It covers object and parameter naming aligned to the layer guidelines, model file organization, level-of-development references, and how to maintain NCS compliance when documents are generated from a model.

title: Symbols & Notations Reference
summary: The complete graphic symbol set and standardized notation conventions for every discipline.
description: A comprehensive, printable reference of the standardized graphic symbols, reference bubbles, and notation conventions used across architectural, structural, mechanical, electrical, and civil drawings.
